title: Rust EDHOC: missing signature verification for Message 2
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 337-369

SECURITY: The Rust EDHOC initiator process_message_2 function decrypts CIPHERTEXT_2 and extracts PLAINTEXT_2, but does NOT verify Signature_2 from the responder. The code proceeds directly from decryption to computing TH_3 without validating that the responder actually signed M_2.

This is a critical security flaw - an attacker could inject a forged Message 2 with an arbitrary signature and the initiator would accept it, leading to man-in-the-middle attacks. Per RFC 9528 Section 4.3.2, the initiator MUST verify Signature_2 before proceeding.

Compare to the Python implementation which attempts verification (though with incorrect API usage - see related issue).
